page: Microsoft Azure F72s_v2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the FSv2 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 72 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 36 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The x86_64 architecture supports hyper-threading, Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX and AVX-512F), and hardware-assisted virtualization. The system is equipped with 144.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage consists of a 590 GB drive, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPU accelerators are present.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core processing and memory bandwidth capabilities. The server achieves a top-tier rating in multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests, alongside high memory bandwidth measurements for both cached small-block reads and uncached large-block read/write operations. Redis SET operations also register in the top 25% tier, reaching 59.39 million operations per second. Conversely, single-core execution speeds, single-threaded compression, and software compilation benchmarks fall within the average tier. Large language model inference tasks show a distinct performance tradeoff, with prompt processing speeds remaining average while text generation speeds for small and medium models drop into the bottom 25% tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F72s_v2 offers high compute density relative to its memory footprint, making it a viable option for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory-to-CPU ratios. The combination of 72 dedicated vCPUs, Premium Storage compatibility, and strong multi-core performance makes this server suitable for multi-threaded batch processing, Redis-based caching layers, web serving, and parallelized mathematical simulations. However, memory-intensive databases or generative AI text generation tasks may be constrained by the 2.0 GB per vCPU memory allocation and the lack of hardware acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure F72s_v2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the FSv2 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 72 dedicated vCPUs and 36 physical cores. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.6 GHz, it features 144.0 GB of memory (2.0 GB per core) and 590 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Benchmark results show strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth, alongside top-tier Redis SET operation throughput. However, single-core performance remains average, and text generation speeds for large language models are weak. This resource profile makes the instance suitable for multi-threaded batch processing, high-throughput web serving, and caching layers, while workloads requiring high memory-to-CPU ratios or GPU acceleration are less aligned with its hardware configuration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F72s_v2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 72 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 144 GB of RAM, and 590 GB of local storage, delivering strong multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F72s_v2 is a compute-optimized VM with 72 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 144 GB RAM, and 590 GB storage, built for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring seventy-two dedicated processor cores and balanced memory bandwidth for parallelized, multi-threaded workloads.

144.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per vCPU
590 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
